sections.paragraph:
ὑπολύω (since Hom. in var. senses) lit. ‘untie’ then take off one’s sandals/shoes (Aeschyl., Aristoph. et al.; LXX; Just., A I, 62, 2 and 4) w. acc. of the pers. whose sandals one takes off τινά (Pla., Symp. 213b; Plut., Pomp. 658 [73, 10]; Jos., Ant. 4, 256 ὑπ. αὐτὸν τὰ σάνδαλα; TestZeb 3:5) ὑπολύειν ἑαυτόν take off one’s own sandals MPol 13:2.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

var. var. = various(ly)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

TestZeb TestZeb = Testament of Zebulon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1039
